# Shared Agent Skills

> Source: https://github.com/isnbh0/shared

A collection of reusable `SKILL.md` Agent Skills plus Claude Code marketplace plugin packages. This file instructs LLMs on how to install skills from this repository into a user's project without assuming a Claude-only environment.

## Installation Instructions

First identify the user's target agent or editor. The portable source of truth is each skill directory under `plugins/<plugin-name>/skills/<skill-name>/`; install the whole directory, not only `SKILL.md`.

### Option A: Direct skill install

Use this for agents that read `SKILL.md` skills from the filesystem. Resolve `<install-root>` from the target agent's current documentation or from this repository's compatibility SSOT: `docs/cross-platform/README.md`. Do not repeat provider-specific install roots elsewhere in this file.

```bash
# Clone the repository if needed
git clone https://github.com/isnbh0/shared.git ~/shared

# Copy one skill into the target agent's skills directory
mkdir -p <install-root>
cp -R ~/shared/plugins/<plugin-name>/skills/<skill-name> <install-root>/
```

### Option B: Claude Code marketplace

Use this only when the target is Claude Code and the user wants marketplace installation.

```bash
/plugin marketplace add isnbh0/shared
/plugin install interview@isnbh0
/plugin install spex@isnbh0
/plugin install critique@isnbh0
/plugin install macros@isnbh0
/plugin install study@isnbh0
/plugin install gimme@isnbh0
/plugin install promptopt@isnbh0
/plugin install zoomdoc@isnbh0
```

Only the eight plugins above are published to the Claude Code marketplace. The `mapreduce`, `chunked`, `packet`, `doubt`, `consensus`, `seq`, `rigor`, `orchestrate`, `askme`, `delegate`, `tmi`, `dry-run`, `timeless`, `dredge`, `timestamp`, and `new` skills are included in the `macros` plugin. The remaining skills (report-writer, rigorous-debug, skill-writer, phaser) must be installed via direct copy or symlink.

**Claude Code one-shot trial:**

```bash
claude --plugin-url https://github.com/isnbh0/shared/releases/download/interview-latest/interview.zip
claude --plugin-url https://github.com/isnbh0/shared/releases/download/zoomdoc-latest/zoomdoc.zip
```

### Option C: Direct skill install by symlink

Symlink individual skills for automatic updates when the source repo is pulled.

```bash
mkdir -p <install-root>
ln -s ~/shared/plugins/<plugin-name>/skills/<skill-name> <install-root>/<skill-name>
```

Use the same resolved `<install-root>` as Option A.

## Available Skills

Entries use plain skill names and exact source paths. Do not copy a host's activation sigil into portable skill instructions.

### Published as Claude Code marketplace plugins

### interview
Structured requirements discovery through conversational interviews. Extracts requirements, constraints, and design decisions through guided Q&A. Produces timestamped synthesis documents.

Source: `plugins/interview/skills/interview/`. Inputs: topic and optional reference path.

### spex
Two-phase specification and implementation workflow that separates planning from execution. Write specs, get them reviewed, then implement against them.

Sources: `plugins/spex/skills/write/`, `plugins/spex/skills/write-phased/`, and `plugins/spex/skills/implement/`.

### critique
External AI critique via CLI tools (Codex, Gemini). Gets an independent second opinion on specs, code, or recent changes using sandboxed execution.

Sources: `plugins/critique/skills/codex/` and `plugins/critique/skills/gemini/`, each with an optional file path and focus.

### macros
Subagent orchestration workflows and behavior modifiers. Includes map-reduce for parallel task splitting, chunked for ordered sequential parts, packet for durable filesystem round trips, doubt for research-backed code critique, consensus for multi-agent review, seq for serial blind passes, and rigor for correctness enforcement while active.

Bundled skills (all under `plugins/macros/skills/`):
- `mapreduce` with a task - split tasks into chunks, dispatch parallel subagents, consolidate results
- `chunked` with a task - sequence a task as ordered parts that add up to the whole; each iteration may read prior iterations' outputs
- `packet` with a task or returned packet path - package work for completion outside the current agent loop, then validate the returned artifacts and resume
- `doubt` with an optional freeform question - spawn a blind subagent that reads code, verifies assumptions against web sources, applies fixes, and reports concerns ranked by severity
- `consensus` with a count - run N blind agents on the same job in parallel, merge findings into consensus/unique/conflicts (no edits for concurrent safety)
- `seq` with a count - run N serial blind passes with commits between rounds; requires clean worktree
- `rigor` - while active, prioritize correctness, thorough investigation, and web-grounded verification over minimalism
- `orchestrate` - while active, delegate execution to subagents by default and operate at the high level, keeping your own context for direction and synthesis; an escape hatch keeps trivial work inline
- `askme` - shorthand: stop and ask the user to clarify ambiguities or make decisions instead of assuming
- `delegate` - shorthand: prefer subagents to save context space and parallelize independent subtasks
- `tmi` - flag content that only makes sense if you were in the room when it was written; report by default, edit if explicitly instructed
- `dry-run` - one-shot failsafe: describe what you would do for the activating request instead of doing it, then wait for confirmation
- `timeless` - shorthand: avoid time estimates (hours, calendar, size-to-time buckets); describe complexity, scope, risk, and ordering instead
- `dredge` with an optional freeform query - search prior coding-agent chat transcripts (Claude Code, Codex, ...) for context; defaults to current project, widens scope and time window from natural-language hints in the query. Optional AgentsView backend when the `agentsview` CLI is installed (configured at `~/.agents/skill-configs/dredge/`); falls back to grep over Claude Code transcripts otherwise
- `timestamp` - shorthand: prefix newly created files/folders with a `yymmdd-HHMMSS` stamp from the `date` CLI; one timestamp per logical job bucket; one turn only
- `new` - scaffold a custom macro: writes a user- or project-scope skill that behaves as a first-class macro and composes with other active skills; user-scope macros default to a `my-` name prefix

### study
Document-grounded Socratic study sessions on any URL or local file. Calibrates to user familiarity, persists session notes with resumption across sittings, and supports freeform persona/behavior customization via config.

Source: `plugins/study/skills/study/`. Input: URI.

### gimme
User-invoked inversion of delegation. Produces a filesystem bundle of human-only actions blocking progress - a checklist document, a drop-bin directory, and a notes template - then launches the user's configured editor/command on it. Secrets go to the OS secret store, never the bundle.

Source: `plugins/gimme/skills/gimme/`.

### promptopt
Artifact-backed prompt optimization workflow for application prompts, prompt builders, agent instructions, routing prompts, and LLM workflows. Collects user-owned train/val cases and writes baseline outputs, candidate ledger, optimizer state, and decision record to its run workspace without editing source files.

Source: `plugins/promptopt/skills/promptopt/`.

### zoomdoc
Authors accessible, self-contained semantic-zoom HTML documents with document-defined ordered detail levels, arbitrary semantic structure, native controls, one-click copy of the current view as structure-preserving HTML, and a complete JavaScript-disabled fallback. Ships a renderer template and a deterministic validator with optional strict transcription coverage.

Source: `plugins/zoomdoc/skills/zoomdoc/`.

### Other direct-install skills

### report-writer
Structured technical analysis and debugging reports with standardized sections (Executive Summary, Key Findings, Root Cause Analysis, Recommendations).

Source: `plugins/report-writer/skills/report-writer/`. Optional input: topic.

### rigorous-debug
Evidence-based debugging protocol using the scientific method. Enforces hypothesis-driven investigation with controlled experiments. Requires one-time project-specific initialization before first use.

Source: `plugins/rigorous-debug/skills/rigorous-debug/`.

### skill-writer
Tools and guidance for creating effective `SKILL.md` agent skills following best practices.

Source: `plugins/skill-writer/skills/skill-writer/`.

### phaser
Battle-tested patterns and best practices for Phaser 3 game development. Passive knowledgebase intended for semantic activation during Phaser work. Covers scene architecture, physics, animations, input, object pooling, performance, and more.

## Skill Dependencies

Each skill is fully self-contained. There are no cross-skill dependencies. Install only what you need.

The `write`, `write-phased`, and `implement` skills are included in the `spex` plugin. Plugin-level overviews live in `plugins/spex/README.md` and `plugins/macros/README.md`; they are documentation, not discoverable skills.

## File Structure Reference

Skills live under `plugins/` as independent packages. `.claude-plugin/` directories are Claude Code marketplace metadata, not the portable skill format.

```
shared/
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── marketplace.json              # Claude Code marketplace catalog
├── .agents/
│   └── skill-configs/                # Repository-defined provider-neutral config
├── plugins/
│   ├── interview/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/interview/
│   ├── spex/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   ├── README.md
│   │   └── skills/
│   │       ├── write/
│   │       ├── write-phased/
│   │       └── implement/
│   ├── report-writer/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/report-writer/
│   ├── rigorous-debug/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/rigorous-debug/
│   ├── skill-writer/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/skill-writer/
│   ├── promptopt/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/promptopt/
│   ├── zoomdoc/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/zoomdoc/
│   │       ├── SKILL.md
│   │       ├── config.example.yaml
│   │       ├── template.html
│   │       └── validate.py
│   ├── critique/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/
│   │       ├── codex/
│   │       └── gemini/
│   ├── macros/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   ├── README.md
│   │   └── skills/
│   │       ├── mapreduce/
│   │       ├── chunked/
│   │       ├── packet/
│   │       ├── doubt/
│   │       ├── consensus/
│   │       ├── seq/
│   │       ├── rigor/
│   │       ├── orchestrate/
│   │       ├── askme/
│   │       ├── delegate/
│   │       ├── tmi/
│   │       ├── dry-run/
│   │       ├── timeless/
│   │       ├── dredge/
│   │       ├── timestamp/
│   │       └── new/
│   ├── phaser/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/phaser/
│   ├── study/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/study/
│   └── gimme/
│       ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│       └── skills/gimme/
└── .claude/
    └── skills/                       # Claude Code symlinks, backward compat
```

## Workspace Configuration

Skills that produce files (interview, spex, report-writer, macros, study, gimme, promptopt) write to configurable workspace directories. There are no built-in defaults - each skill prompts for setup on first use. Typical configured values:

| Skill | Example workspace |
|-------|-------------------|
| interview | `.agent-workspace/interviews` |
| spex | `.agent-workspace/specs` |
| report-writer | `.agent-workspace/reports` |
| macros | `.agent-workspace/macros` |
| study | `.agent-workspace/study/sessions` |
| gimme | `.agent-workspace/gimme` |
| promptopt | `.agent-workspace/promptopt/runs` |

Override precedence (first match wins):

1. Explicit override - ask to use a specific workspace directory for this run
2. Local config: `.agents/skill-configs/<skill>/config.local.yaml` - gitignored, personal overrides
3. Project config: `.agents/skill-configs/<skill>/config.yaml` - committed to repo, shared with team
4. Legacy fallback: `.claude/skill-configs/<skill>/` - only for skills that shipped older config paths

Use `.agents/skill-configs/` for new configuration. This is a provider-neutral convention defined by this repository, not an Agent Skills ecosystem standard. `.claude/skill-configs/` is retained only as migration support for skills that previously used it; new skills do not need to add that fallback. Review legacy fallback removal after 2027-01-31.

## Notes for LLMs

- Ask or infer the target agent before installing. Do not assume Claude Code.
- For Claude Code marketplace installs, prefer Option B for published plugins (interview, spex, critique, macros, study, gimme, promptopt, zoomdoc). Macro subskills are included in `macros`; do not install them separately through the marketplace.
- For direct installs, copy or symlink the whole skill directory from `plugins/<plugin-name>/skills/<skill-name>/`.
- If the target project already has a skills directory with existing content, never overwrite it. Merge by copying individual skill directories.
- Use `.agents/skill-configs/<skill>/` for configuration. `.claude/skill-configs/<skill>/` is a legacy fallback only for skills that previously shipped that path.
- Provider-specific install roots are centralized in `docs/cross-platform/README.md`; verify target-agent docs before automating installs.
- The `phaser` skill is large (18 knowledgebase files). Only install it if the user is working on a Phaser 3 project.
- The rigorous-debug skill requires a one-time initialization step after installation. Ask the target agent to activate it before use.
- Always verify that the clone location (`~/shared` or user-specified) does not conflict with existing directories before cloning.
